channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Race for DNC chair narrows after longshot candidate drops out, endorses Minnesota's Ken Martin
item.description: New York state Sen. James Skoufis dropped out of the race for DNC chair and endorsed Ken Martin, Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party chair.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How would RFK Jr. handle bird flu? His record on vaccines has experts on edge
item.description: Trump's pick for health secretary has a record of attacking vaccines. In fact he's even taken direct aim at bird flu shots. Some scientists fear this doesn't bode well in case of a crisis.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Newsom joins handful of GOP governors raising flags to full height for Trump inauguration
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has ordered that flags at the state Capitol temporarily fly at full-staff for the inauguration of President-elect Donald Trump on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Local reactions mixed as Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal moves forward
item.description: The Middle East and the world are celebrating the Israel-Hamas ceasefire as a possible step toward peace, but many are still cautious about the deal.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats' crisis of the future: the biggest states that back them are shrinking
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Texas and Florida are growing rapidly. California, Illinois and New York are shrinking. With America's population shifting to the South, political influence is seeping from reliably Democratic states to areas controlled by Republicans.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER and KEVIN S. VINEYS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five things to know about Biden's farewell address that also served as a warning to the country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With only days left in the White House, President Joe Biden was saving a few surprises for his farewell address Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok ban to impact local businesses, content creators
item.description: San Diego is home to talented and creative content creators who make a living on TikTok. While many are able to make a full-time income on the app, some are also helping small businesses in return, who say the app has inadvertently helped them tremendously.
item.dc:creator: Zara Barker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's outsider Cabinet picks are rapidly gaining support for confirmation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — One by one, all the president-elect’ s men, and women, are falling into place in his Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Murphy calling for cellphone ban in NJ classrooms
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (PIX11) - Could there be a day when there will be no cellphones in New Jersey classrooms? In his State of the State address on Tuesday, Gov. Phil Murphy announced his support for legislation that would direct school districts to adopt policies that ban cellphones in K-12 classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Winners and losers from the Texas House speaker’s race
item.description: The new speaker’s power over the budget and committee assignments is expected to bolster established Republican leaders and some Democrats who supported the winner.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy, an Ohio native, is interested in filling Vance's old Senate seat, AP source says
item.description: Former Republican presidential candidate Vivek Ramaswamy has expressed interest in the Ohio U.S. Senate seat that was vacated by Vice President-elect JD Vance, two people with direct knowledge of the biotech entrepreneur's wishes told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pam Bondi grilled over Trump's influence at AG hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pam Bondi, President-elect Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, echoed attacks on the agency she would lead if confirmed, but demurred on how she might handle tests of its independence. Bondi is a more traditional pick to lead the department than Trump’s initial choice, former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: End to daylight saving time among bills for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: A Republican lawmaker who represents parts of six rural Nevada counties is sponsoring a bill to take the state off daylight saving time — one of the 200 bills written so far.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's pick for budget director won't say whether he would release Ukraine aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russell Vought, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for White House budget director, declined Wednesday to fully commit to doling out congressionally approved funds, specifically U.S. military aid to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the special election for Vargas' Board of Supervisors seat
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors on Tuesday finalized plans to fill the seat formerly held by Nora Vargas before her abrupt exit from elected office through a special election.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Just 2 in 10 confident Trump DOJ will act fairly: Survey
item.description: GOP voters are divided when it comes to their faith in the DOJ being neutral even with the incoming Republican administration.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: About 2 in 10 Americans back Hegseth as Trump's Defense secretary: Survey
item.description: In the poll from The Associated Press-NORC Research Center, 19 percent of respondents said that they “strongly approve” or “somewhat approve” the selection of Hegseth by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Government agencies concerned Trump inauguration 'potential target' for extremists: report
item.description: Agencies are concerned that violent extremists may be drawn to President-elect Trump's inauguration, particularly those with "election-related grievances," according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats and Republicans criticize Biden admin's Cuba detente
item.description: The Biden administration earned blowback from both Democrats and Republicans for moving to nix Cuba's state sponsor of terrorism designation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip the inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Biden promised to turn the page on Trump. Now he's being replaced by him
item.description: Joe Biden staked his presidency on the idea that he was the best person to keep Donald Trump out of power.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: U.S. attorneys for Michigan resign before Trump takes office
item.description: Both of Michigan's U.S. attorneys have announced they will resign from their posts before Donald Trump is sworn into office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: NY plan would make community college free: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to make community college free for adults pursuing in-demand careers.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Hochul's tax cut plan: What it means for New Yorkers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to cut taxes for more than 8.3 million taxpayers in New York. The proposal was announced as part of her 2025 State of the State address.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: No. 2 Senate Democratic leader will back Rubio for secretary of State
item.description: Senate Democratic Whip Dick Durbin (Ill.) on Monday announced that he will vote for Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to become the next secretary of State, a sign that Rubio is on a path to be confirmed on President-elect Trump’s first day of office with overwhelming support.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo raises $3.4 million in 2024 for re-election bid
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is off to a strong start in building campaign funds, raising about $3.4 million over the past year, according to mandatory campaign filing reports.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Special counsel in Hunter Biden case denounces president for criticism of the probe
item.description: In a final report on his investigation, special counsel David Weiss said President Biden's criticism "undermines the very foundation of what makes America's justice system fair and equitable.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Trump is planning 100 executive orders starting Day 1 on border, deportations and other priorities
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is preparing more than 100 executive orders starting Day One of the new White House, in what amounts to a shock-and-awe campaign on border security, deportations and a rush of other policy priorities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5
